questions.q: Kanchipuram served as the capital of which dynasty?
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram was the capital of the Pallava dynasty.

questions.q: Kanchipuram is located in which present-day Indian state?
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram lies in present-day Tamil Nadu.

questions.q: Kanchipuram is most famous as a city of what?
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram is renowned as a city of temples, earning the subtitle 'City of Temples'.

questions.q: Besides temples, Kanchipuram is famous for producing which textile?
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram is celebrated for its fine silk weaving and Kanchipuram silk sarees.

questions.q: Kanchipuram has long been revered as one of India's what?
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram is one of the seven traditional holy cities (sapta puri) of India.

questions.q: Under the Pallavas, Kanchipuram was a major centre of which activity besides temple-building?
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram was a major centre of learning, trade, and temple-building.

questions.q: Kanchipuram is an ancient city associated with which broad faith tradition's temples?
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram is renowned for its Hindu temples, both Shaiva and Vaishnava.

questions.q: The Pallava king Rajasimha built which famous temple at Kanchipuram?
questions.explanation: The Kailasanatha temple at Kanchipuram was built by the Pallava king Rajasimha.

questions.q: Kanchipuram is often referred to by which short popular name?
questions.explanation: The city is commonly known by its short form, Kanchi.

questions.q: Kanchipuram lay within which broad region of ancient India?
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram is in the Tamil region of southern India.

questions.q: Kanchipuram is traditionally counted among the seven sacred cities (Sapta Puri) believed to grant what?
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram is one of the seven holy cities said to bestow moksha, or spiritual liberation.

questions.q: The great Vaishnava temple at Kanchipuram dedicated to Vishnu is which?
questions.explanation: The Varadaraja Perumal temple is the major Vaishnava shrine of Kanchipuram, one of the Divya Desams.

questions.q: The prominent Shaiva temple at Kanchipuram, important to the goddess Kamakshi, is the Ekambareswarar temple, associated with which element?
questions.explanation: The Ekambareswarar temple represents the earth element among the Pancha Bhuta Sthalams of Shiva.

questions.q: Kanchipuram is sometimes divided into two parts associated with Shiva and Vishnu; what are they called?
questions.explanation: The city is traditionally divided into Shiva Kanchi and Vishnu Kanchi, centred on its great temples.

questions.q: After the Pallavas, Kanchipuram came under the control of which dynasty that rose in the Tamil land?
questions.explanation: The Cholas absorbed Kanchipuram after defeating the Pallavas in the late 9th century CE.

questions.q: The famous Kamakshi Amman temple of Kanchipuram is dedicated to which form of the goddess?
questions.explanation: The Kamakshi Amman temple is dedicated to Kamakshi, a form of Parvati and a major Shakti shrine.

questions.q: Kanchipuram was an important centre of which religious traditions besides Hinduism in ancient times?
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram was also a notable centre of Buddhism and Jainism in the early medieval period.

questions.q: The Chinese pilgrim Xuanzang visited Kanchipuram and noted its prominence as a centre of which faith at that time?
questions.explanation: Xuanzang recorded Kanchipuram as a flourishing centre with many Buddhist monasteries in the 7th century.

questions.q: Kanchipuram silk sarees are prized for being woven from which kind of material?
options: Mulberry silk with gold zari thread
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram (Kanjeevaram) sarees are woven from pure mulberry silk with gold zari thread.

questions.q: The Kailasanatha temple of Kanchipuram is an important early example of which architectural style?
questions.explanation: The Kailasanatha temple is a landmark of early Dravidian temple architecture under the Pallavas.

questions.q: Kanchipuram later became the seat of an important monastic institution (matha) traditionally linked to which philosopher?
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram is the seat of the Kanchi Kamakoti Peetham, traditionally associated with Adi Shankara.

questions.q: Kanchipuram's strategic and cultural importance meant it was contested between the Pallavas and which Deccan rivals?
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram was repeatedly contested between the Pallavas and the Chalukyas of Badami.

questions.q: Kanchipuram was praised in classical Sanskrit literature as among the foremost of cities. Which Sri Vaishnava (Vishishtadvaita) tradition is closely associated with the city?
options: Ramanuja's tradition (Kanchipurna and the Vaishnava acharyas)
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram is closely linked to the Sri Vaishnava tradition, including figures around Ramanuja such as Kanchipurna.

questions.q: The Vaikunta Perumal temple at Kanchipuram, built by Nandivarman II, is notable for its sculptured panels depicting what?
options: The history of the Pallava dynasty
The life of the Buddha only
questions.explanation: The Vaikunta Perumal temple has unique relief panels narrating the genealogy and history of the Pallavas.

questions.q: Kanchipuram is one of the 108 Divya Desams; how many of these sacred Vishnu shrines are located in the city, making it especially significant for Sri Vaishnavas?
questions.explanation: Kanchipuram contains around fourteen of the 108 Divya Desams, the highest concentration in one city.

questions.q: The early name of Kanchipuram appearing in Sangam-era and Pallava sources was which?
questions.explanation: The city was known in Tamil sources as Kanchi (Kacchi), long predating the medieval temples.

questions.q: Kanchipuram's importance as a Buddhist centre is reflected in the tradition that which great Buddhist logician was associated with the city?
options: Dignaga (and traditions linking Bodhidharma to the region)
questions.explanation: The Buddhist logician Dignaga is traditionally linked to Kanchipuram, and some legends connect Bodhidharma to the area.

questions.q: Under the Cholas, Kanchipuram was administered as part of a larger province; the city retained importance for which royal activity?
questions.explanation: Under the Cholas, Kanchipuram remained a key centre of temple patronage and provincial administration.

questions.q: In later centuries Kanchipuram passed through the hands of the Vijayanagara Empire and then which power before the colonial era?
options: The Nayaks and later the Carnatic Nawabs
questions.explanation: After Vijayanagara, Kanchipuram came under the Nayaks and later the Nawabs of the Carnatic before British rule.

questions.q: The Ekambareswarar temple at Kanchipuram is famed for an ancient sacred tree under which the deity is said to be worshipped; what tree is it?
questions.explanation: The Ekambareswarar temple is associated with a revered ancient mango tree (sthala vriksham).
